title: Travel Therapist and Allied-Health Housing in Cleveland
metaTitle: Travel Therapist Housing in Cleveland | Allied Health
metaDescription: Travel therapist housing in Cleveland for PT, OT, SLP, imaging, and lab travelers, matched to your facility commute. Send dates and location to our local team.
secondaryKeywords: pt ot slp travel assignment housing
answerBox: Travel therapist housing in Cleveland is easiest to sort by commute to your assigned facility. Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team with 90-plus homes across the East and Southeast side, some nearby suburbs, and Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. Send your assignment dates and the hospital or clinic address, and we will tell you honestly what in our current selection fits.

sections.h2: Sort travel therapist housing in Cleveland by your commute
body: A 13-week assignment leaves little time to learn a new city, so the most useful way to shortlist **travel therapist housing in Cleveland** is by drive time to the facility you were placed at, not by neighborhood name. Tell us the hospital, clinic, or lab, and we work outward from there.

Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team, not a national relocation vendor or a corporate-housing chain. We help renters find, tour, and apply for homes we work with. Our current selection runs about 90-plus homes concentrated on Cleveland's East and Southeast side, some nearby suburbs, and Akron, Lorain, and Elyria.

sections.h2: Match a home to your assigned facility
body: Allied-health travelers -- physical therapists (PT), occupational therapists (OT), speech-language pathologists (SLP), plus imaging techs (X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound) and lab scientists -- get placed all over Greater Cleveland. A few anchor points help frame the drive:

items: Cleveland Clinic main campus and University Circle facilities (University Hospitals) sit on the East side, near many of the homes we work with.
MetroHealth's main campus is on the near West side; ask and we'll be honest about which of our homes make that commute reasonable.
Akron-area assignments (Cleveland Clinic Akron General, Summa Health) pair well with our Akron selection.
Lorain and Elyria placements (Mercy Health, University Hospitals in Elyria) line up with homes we work with in those cities.

sections.h2: What to send us: your dates and location
body: The fastest way to get a real answer is a short message with the specifics. The more you include, the sooner we can point you to actual options in [our current selection](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/).

items: Assignment start and end dates, or expected length
The facility name or address you'll commute to
Bedrooms needed and how many people, plus any pets
Your monthly housing budget or stipend range
Whether you need furnished or can bring your own furniture

body: Most homes we work with lease as standard, unfurnished rentals, and many are set up for longer terms rather than a strict 13-week furnished stay. If a fully furnished, short-term unit is a hard requirement, we'll tell you up front rather than burn your assignment window on a home that can't work.

Every home we work with also welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept HUD-inspection-ready. Most travelers simply pay market rent, but if a household member uses a voucher, [that is no obstacle](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

One local detail: Cleveland itself has no source-of-income ordinance, though a handful of inner-ring suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale) do. It rarely affects a market-rate traveler, but we welcome vouchers everywhere we operate and follow the Fair Housing Act in every interaction.

sections.h2: For staffing agencies and housing coordinators
body: If you place PT, OT, SLP, imaging, or lab travelers into Greater Cleveland, [tell us your team's needs](/corporate-leasing/) and we'll see how we can help. We're a local rental team, so we won't over-promise a nationwide furnished-housing program we don't run -- but for assignments near the areas we cover, we can move quickly on tours and applications.

Send a batch of upcoming placements with dates and facilities, and we'll flag what in our current selection could be a reasonable commute and what clearly won't -- straight drive-time math, no runaround.

Once something looks like a fit, you can [book a showing](/p/book-showing) in person, or ask about a remote walkthrough if you're finishing a prior assignment out of state. Standard tenant application steps follow once you choose a home.
Questions before you send dates? Call **(440) 444-4737** or email **support@rentfindercleveland.com** and a local person will answer.

faq.q: Do you offer furnished, short-term rentals for a 13-week assignment?
faq.a: Not usually. Most homes we work with are standard, unfurnished leases, and some are geared to longer terms. Send your dates and we'll tell you honestly whether anything in our current selection fits a short assignment.

faq.q: How do I find housing near my assigned Cleveland hospital?
faq.a: Send us the facility name or address plus your dates. We work outward by drive time and tell you what in our current selection is a reasonable commute, especially on the East and Southeast side and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria.

faq.q: Are you a corporate-housing or relocation company?
faq.a: No. We're a local rental team that helps renters find, tour, and apply for homes we work with. We don't run a national relocation program, so we'll be straight about what we can and can't do.

faq.q: Can my staffing agency send multiple placements at once?
faq.a: Yes. Send a list of upcoming assignments with dates and facilities, and we'll flag which locations line up with the areas we cover and which don't.

faq.q: Do I need a Housing Choice Voucher to rent from you?
faq.a: No. Every home we work with welcomes vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, but most travelers simply pay market rent. Voucher or not, the standard application steps are the same.
